title: trigger formatFromDisplay

trigger formatFromDisplay

trigger formatFromDisplay

System trigger that temporarily converts the format of data in a field from an external format into an internal format, and makes that reformatted data available for processing purposes.

Declaration:

trigger formatFromDisplay

Applies to: Field
Activation: Activated when the user tries to leave the field, when the field is referenced in ProcScript, or when a ^DETAIL, ^HELP, or ^MENU structure editor function is used. For details, see Trigger Activation.
Default behavior: None
Behavior upon completion: If $status is less than zero, the data is not transferred from $format to the field and the user cannot leave the field; no further triggers are activated.

Description

The formatFromDisplay trigger is used to convert external data (in a component field) into the internal data format used by Uniface. The formatToDisplay trigger should redo the deformatting done by formatFromDisplay.

The formatToDisplay and formatFromDisplay triggers are especially useful with custom widgets. For widgets, ValRep mapping happens after formatToDisplay and before formatFromDisplay.

When formatFromDisplay is activated, $format contains the formatted data. You can use 3GL or ProcScript to convert this data. When the trigger completes, $format should contain the data ready for storage; this must be in the required syntax as defined in the syntax definition to avoid an error situation. Any subsequent operations performed on the data by Uniface will be on the internal format data in $format.

This trigger is the only trigger that allows the developer to access the raw external data entered by the user. In any other trigger, references to the field refer to the internal format data.

CAUTION: The formatFromDisplay trigger should only be used for deformatting of the associated field. Do not use this trigger for deformatting other fields or for other field-related processing.

Trigger Activation

The formatFromDisplay trigger is activated in the following circumstances:

  • When the user tries to leave the field, before any syntax checks for the field and before the field's loseFocus trigger.
  • Whenever the field is referenced in ProcScript code.
  • When a ^DETAIL, ^HELP, or ^MENU structure editor function is used, before the corresponding detail, help, or menu trigger.

In all cases, the formatToDisplay trigger is activated after the formatFromDisplay trigger to restore the value of $format.

Related Links
  • trigger formatToDisplay

    System trigger that temporarily converts the format of data in a field from an internal format into an external format that is displayed or printed.
